prose:
Fukui reactivity indices computed for benzaldehyde (14 atoms: O, C×7, H×6) using DFT at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level of theory (tier 'standard') in PySCF, gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Exact four-center integrals were used (density fitting was NOT enabled). The N±1 states were computed as doublets (charge +1/−1, multiplicity 2). Partial charges were obtained via the Mulliken scheme. Geometry was taken from the user-supplied .xyz (no optimization performed). All integrity checks passed (status: ok, trustworthy: true); sum(f+) = 1.0000, sum(f−) = 1.0000. No warnings were reported.

Headline result — most electrophilic site: Atom 3 (C, carbonyl carbon), f⁺ = 0.39938965. Most nucleophilic site: Atom 1 (O, carbonyl oxygen), f⁻ = 0.23839906 (both fully consistent with expected benzaldehyde reactivity).

Full per-atom Fukui indices (f+, f−, f⁰, dual descriptor) are reported in the table above.

CLI: /home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py fukui [input.xyz] --method dft --tier standard --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --charge 0 --mult 1
Output: benzaldehyde_fukui_dft.json
Plot: .../agent_call_01_plot.png
